input.description: Map Angular itemDetail component
input.prompt:
Explore the Angular app at D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend to map the existing **item detail** page so it can be redesigned. I need a thorough report. Investigate VERY thoroughly across multiple locations.

Find and report on:

1. **The itemDetail component**: Look under `src/app/pages/itemDetail/` (and anywhere similar like `item-detail`, `itemMaster`). Report the exact folder path and list its files (.ts, .html, .scss). For the component .ts file, quote: the @Component decorator (selector, templateUrl, styleUrls), the class properties (especially the item data model object and its field names), how it loads data (service calls, route params, ngOnInit), and the methods. Show me the real field names on the item object (e.g. itemNum, productType, etc.) so I can map the design's sample fields to real ones.

2. **The HTML template**: Summarize how it currently renders (~40 fields in flat rows across two sub-tabs per the design brief). Quote representative chunks showing the field bindings and the two sub-tab structure. Note what UI library is used (Angular Material? mat-tab? fxLayout? plain CSS grid?).

3. **Routing**: Find the route definition that maps to this component (e.g. `/apps/itemDetail/:id` or similar). Quote the route path and how the :id param is read.

4. **The data service & model**: Find the service that fetches the item and any TypeScript interface/model/class for the item. Quote the field names and types. I specifically need the real field names for: identity (item number, reference, art number, display name, description, division), merch-group codes (the MG01-MG15 fields — product type, sub-type, property, licensor, style guide, size, art source, artist, age group, for customer, etc.), specs (width, depth, weight, UOM, packaging, cost, lead time, vendor, note, season, product manager), status fields, dates, team/assignments, tags, attachments, comments.

5. **MG-code schema**: Search the codebase for "MG01", "MG02"... "MG15", "merchGroup", "merch_group", "mg_code" or similar. The design assumes MG01=productType, MG02=productSubType, MG03=productSubSubType, MG04=size, MG05=licensor, MG06=property, MG07=styleGuide, MG08=artSource, MG09=artist, MG10=ageGroup, MG15=forCustomer. Confirm or correct these against the real schema — report exactly what you find (field names, labels, any mapping table/config).

6. **Licensing & Calendar**: Find any existing licensing / license-tracking / license-stage-tracker component and any calendar/key-dates component related to items. Report paths and the data shape they consume.

7. **Styling/theming conventions**: How does this app do styling? SCSS with variables? A theme file? CSS custom properties already defined? Where is the global styles file? Is there an existing design-token/SCSS-variables file I should extend? What fonts are loaded (index.html / angular.json)? Report the global styles path and any existing color/spacing variables.

8. **Icon library**: What icons does the app use (mat-icon? a custom SVG set? font icons?). Report how icons are rendered so I can match the design's line-icon set.

9. **Component conventions**: standalone components vs NgModules? What Angular version (check package.json)? Are there shared/reusable UI components (cards, pills, badges) already? Where do shared components live?

Report concrete file paths (relative to designflow-frontend) and quote real field names and code. Do NOT modify anything. Be exhaustive — this drives a full page rebuild.

content.text: # Designflow ItemDetail Component Exploration Report ## 1. Component Location & Files **Path:** `src/app/pages/itemDetail/` **Files:** - `itemDetail.component.ts` (880 lines) - Main component class - `itemDetail.component.html` (1336 lines) - Template with 3 nested tabs + licensing/calendar tabs - `itemDetail.component.scss` (355 lines) - Styling with Material Design and custom layouts - `itemDetail-routing.module.ts` - Route definition - `itemDetail.module.ts` - NgModule declarations --- ## 2. The ItemDetail Component: TypeScript Class **@Component Decorator:** ```typescript @Component({ selector: "app-itemDetail", templateUrl: "./itemDetail.component.html", styleUrls: ["./itemDetail.component.scss"], standalone: false // Using NgModule, not standalone }) ``` **Route Path:** `/apps/itemDetail/:item` (from app-routing.module.ts line 177) - The `:item` parameter is read from the URL as `itemDetailId` via `document.location.href.split("/").pop()` **Key Class Properties (Data Model):** The core item data object is `itemData: any = []` (line 101, indexed as `itemData[0]` when loaded). **Real Field Names on Item Object:** ```typescript // Identity Fields item_num_id // Style number (key identifier) ref_num // Reference number (when no style number) dsn_ref_num // Art number / design reference art_piece_id // Link to art-piece component item_displ_descr_name // Display description (shown in header) item_descr_name // Item description (editable) item_content // Item content field div_code_fk // Division code (1=POP Lic, 2/9=Spruce, 7=POP Lic, 8=Spruce Lic) // MG Code Fields (Merchandise Group - see section 5 for mappings) udf_merchgroup01 // Product Type (MG01) udf_merchgroup01_id // Product Type ID udf_merchgroup02 // Product Sub-Type (MG02) udf_merchgroup02_id // Product Sub-Type ID udf_merchgroup03 // Product Sub-Sub-Type (MG03) udf_merchgroup03_id // Product Sub-Sub-Type ID udf_merchgroup04 // Size (MG04) udf_merchgroup04_id // Size ID udf_merchgroup05_fk // Licensor (MG05) OR Big Theme (if div_code=9) udf_merchgroup05_fk_id // Licensor/Big Theme ID udf_merchgroup06_fk // Property (MG06) OR Little Theme (if div_code=9) udf_merchgroup06_fk_id // Property/Little Theme ID udf_merchgroup07_fk // Style Guide (MG07) OR Art Type (if div_code=9) udf_merchgroup08_fk // Art Source (MG08) udf_merchgroup08_fk_id // Art Source ID udf_merchgroup09_fk // Artist (MG09) udf_merchgroup09_fk_id // Artist ID udf_merchgroup10_fk // Age Group (MG10) udf_merchgroup10_fk_id // Age Group ID udf_merchgroup15_fk // For Customer (MG15) - stored as customer name udf_merchgroup15_fk_id // For Customer ID udf_merchgroup11_fk // Style Guide alternatives array // Spec & Packaging Fields item_width_size // Item width item_depth_size // Item depth (selected from depthOptions) item_weight_size // Item weight item_note // Item note / specs note uom_size_fk // Measurement UOM uom_weight_fk // Weight UOM item_cost_ext // Standard cost mfg_lead_time // Manufacturing lead time vendor_code_fk // Vendor foreign key udf_freeform_06 // Packaging type (dropdown of predefined values) // Status Fields item_active_status // Active status (Y/N) discont_status // Discontinued status (Y/N) season_code_fk // Season code productmanager // Product manager name salesper_code_fk // Salesperson code (currently hardcoded "Adam") // Date Fields (mapped to dates[] array) udf_date01 // Earliest on Shelf date udf_date02 // Test Report Recv Date udf_date03 // Photography Recv Date udf_date05 // Fty Sent for Testing udf_date06 // Fty Sent for Photo created_time_date // Created time (index [5] in dates array) due_date // Due date (index [6]) // Additional Fields tags // CSV string of keyword tags (split/joined in component) udf_freeform_02 // Production status (displayed in header) udf_freeform_03 // Brand Assurance udf_freeform_05 // Cust Designed field udf_freeform_07 // Mold Paid Inv udf_freeform_08 // Contr Smpl PO udf_freeform_09 // Annu Smpl PO udf_item_priceA, B, C, D // Item prices (pricing tab) selling_price // Selling price retail_price // Retail price carton_length_size // Carton dimensions carton_width_size carton_depth_size carton_weight_size innerpack_qty // Inner pack quantity udf_int01, udf_int02 // UDF integer fields udfnum01 // UDF numeric field hts_num_ext_fk // HTS number royalty_code_fk // Royalty code mod_user_fk // Modified by user is_item_old // Flag: whether item is old (affects MG selection logic) productUserAssignments // Array of product team assignments // Images & Attachments itemAttachments // Array of file attachments ``` **Component Initialization (ngAfterViewInit/ngOnInit):** ```typescript async ngOnInit() { // 1. Get item ID from URL: /apps/itemDetail/:item this.itemDetailId = href[href.length - 1].split('?')[0] // 2. Load division code (calls getDivisionCode() which fetches itemData) const divisionCode = await this.getDivisionCode() // 3. Load MG codes grouped by type (Material, Construction, Feature, Size, Licensor, // Property, Big Theme, Little Theme, Style guide, Art Type, Art Source, Artist, Age Group) await this.itemService.getMerchGroup({ divisionCode }) // 4. Load item depth options, seasonal codes, company codes, vendors, UDF elements await this.getItemDepth() // 5. Call getItemInfo() to populate all dropdowns with current values await this.getItemInfo() // 6. Set up async tabs array (Main tab + Licensing (if not div 9) + Calendar) } ``` **Key Methods:** - `getDivisionCode()` - Fetches itemData from ItemDetailService via `getItemInfo({ id })` - `getItemInfo()` - Populates all selected* properties by matching item data to loaded options - `onSave()` - Marshals form values back to itemData object and calls `ItemDetailService.updateData()` - `onStyleNumber()` - Requests a new style number via `ItemDetailService.reqStyeNumber()` - `onProductAssignmentChange(role, event)` - Saves team member assignments - `loadProductAssignmentUsers()` - Fetches all users for product team dropdowns --- ## 3. HTML Template Structure **Layout Overview:** The template uses Angular Material (`mat-*`) components with `fxLayout` (FlexLayout) for responsive grid layouts. **Header Card (Top):** ```html Style #: {{data.item_num_id}} ``` **Main Tab Group (mat-tab-group with 3 nested content tabs):** **Tab 0 - "First" (Main Merchandising Tab):** - Read-only view: Displays ~18 fields in flex-wrap grid (2 columns per row) - Edit view: Same fields as mat-form-field inputs + editable tags - Fields shown: Active Status, Item Description, Product Type (MG01), Property (MG06), Product Sub-Type (MG02), Style Guide (MG07), Product Sub-Sub-Type (MG03), Item Content, Size (MG04), Brand Assurance, Season, Product Manager, Licensor (MG05), For Customer (MG15), Art Number reference **Tab 1 - "Second" (Specs & Dates Tab):** - Read-only view: Displays ~24 spec/date fields - Edit view: Inputs for dimensions, weights, dates (with mat-datepicker), vendor selection - Fields: Active Status, Discontinued Status, Item Width/Depth/Weight, UOMs, Item Note, Art Source (MG08), Packaging Type, Artist (MG09), Standard Cost, Age Group (MG10), Date fields (Earliest on Shelf, Due Date, Test Report, Photography, Testing/Photo Send), Mfg Lead Time, Vendor, Art Number **Tab 2 - "Third" (Pricing & Logistics Tab):** - Pricing: Item Prices A/B/C/D, Selling Price, Retail Price - Carton dimensions and weight, Inner pack qty - HTS Number, Royalty Code, Sales Person, Mold Paid Inv, various UDF fields, Created Time, Mod User **Two-Row Header Sub-Components (Right of main image):** 1. **Comments Card** - `` component 2. **Tech Pack Card** - Upload/View techpack files 3. **Tags Card** - Displays tags as chips (if not editing), or as editable chip-grid with ENTER to add 4. **Product Team Card** - 5 role dropdowns (Creative Designer, Creative Director, Technical Designer, Licensing, Salesperson) **Tabs 1+ (Conditional Tabs):** - **Licensing Tab** (tab.id='1'): Shows `` component (if div_code != 9) - **Calendar Tab** (tab.id='2'): Shows `` component with key dates **Edit/Save Buttons:** - Read-only: "Edit" button + "Request a style number" / "Update Item Details" button - Edit mode: "Save" and "Close" buttons --- ## 4. Data Service & Model **Service File:** `src/app/helpers/services/item_detail.service.ts` **Key Methods:** ```typescript getItemInfo(id: any) // POST /detail/getItemInfo updateData(data: any) // POST /detail/updateData reqStyeNumber(data: any) // POST /detail/reqStyeNumber getComments(id: number) // GET /detail/getComments/:id addComment(comment: CommentInterface) // POST /detail/addComment UpdateLicensingStatus(data) // POST /detail/UpdateLicensingStatus GetLicensingStatus(id) // GET /detail/GetLicensingStatus/:id getProductAssignments(itemId) // GET /detail/assignments/:itemId setProductAssignments(itemId, assignments) // POST /detail/assignments/:itemId ``` **Type Definitions:** ```typescript export type ProductAssignmentRole = | 'creative_designer' | 'creative_director' | 'technical_designer' | 'licensing_person' | 'salesperson'; export interface ProductAssignmentUser { id: number; name: string; lastname: string; email: string; profile_photo?: string | null; level?: string; } export interface ProductAssignment { id: number; item_id_fk: number; role: ProductAssignmentRole; user_id_fk: number; user?: ProductAssignmentUser; } ``` **Main Service:** `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` **Key Methods for ItemDetail:** ```typescript getMerchGroup(data: { divisionCode }) // POST /getMerchGroup getSeasonCode() // GET /getSeasonCode getCompanyCode() // GET /getCompanyCode getAllVendors() // GET /getAllVendors getItemDepth() // GET /findItemDepth getCustomers() // GET /findCustomers getUDFElements(data) // POST /getUDFElement ``` --- ## 5. MG-Code (Merchandise Group) Schema **Real Schema Mapping (NOT as assumed in design brief):** The actual code loads MG codes from the backend grouped by **mgTypeDesc** (not by numeric code). The component maps them to the following logical purposes: | Display Label | Field Name | Backend mgTypeDesc | Component Property | Notes | |---|---|---|---|---| | Product Type | udf_merchgroup01 | "Material" | selectedProductType | MG01 logically | | Product Sub-Type | udf_merchgroup02 | "Construction" | selectedProductSubType | MG02 logically | | Product Sub-Sub-Type | udf_merchgroup03 | "Feature" | selectedProductSubSubType | MG03 logically | | Size | udf_merchgroup04 | "Size" | selectedSize | MG04 logically | | Licensor (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup05_fk | "Licensor" | selectedLicensor | MG05 logically | | Big Theme (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup05_fk | "Big Theme" | selectedBigTheme | MG05 alternative for div=9 | | Property (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup06_fk | "Property" | selectedProperty | MG06 logically | | Little Theme (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup06_fk | "Little Theme" | selectedLittleTheme | MG06 alternative for div=9 | | Style Guide (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup07_fk | "Style guide" | selectedStyleGuide | MG07 logically | | Art Type (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup07_fk | "Art Type" | selectedArtType | MG07 alternative for div=9 | | Art Source | udf_merchgroup08_fk | "Art Source" | selectedArtSource | MG08 logically | | Artist | udf_merchgroup09_fk | "Artist" | selectedArtist | MG09 logically | | Age Group | udf_merchgroup10_fk | "Age Group" | selectedAgeGroup | MG10 logically | | For Customer | udf_merchgroup15_fk | (custom/customer table) | selectedForCustomer | MG15 logically | **Database Field Structure for Each MG:** ```typescript { mg_id: number, // Primary key mg_code: string, // Short code (used in dropdowns) mgCode2: string, // Alternative code (displayed in dropdowns) mg_desc: string, // Full description/title is_active: boolean, // Active flag parent_id?: number, // Parent category ID divisionCode_id_fk: number, // Division FK (for filtering by division) createdTime: Date, // Created timestamp (year extracted for old-item logic) mgTypeDesc: string // Type descriptor (Material, Construction, etc.) } ``` **Division-Specific Behavior:** - **Division 9 (Spruce non-Lic)**: Uses Big Theme, Little Theme, Art Type fields instead of Licensor, Property, Style Guide - **Other divisions**: Use standard Licensor, Property, Style Guide fields **Old Item Logic (lines 500-507):** - If `is_item_old === true`, MG selection matches by `mg_code` instead of `id` - Filters to inactive items NOT created in 2025 - Allows showing historical MG codes no longer in the active list --- ## 6. Licensing & Calendar Components **Licensing Component:** - **Path:** `src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/` - **Files:** - `license-tracking.component.ts` - `licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.ts` - Tracks phases (Submission, Resubmission, PPS Approval) - `licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.html` - `licensing_tracking_button/licensing_tracking_button.component.ts` - `feedback_dialog/feedback_dialog.component.ts` **Licensing Stage Tracker Data Shape:** ```typescript interface LicensingStageRow { phase: string; // 'Submission', 'Resubmission', 'PPS Approval' status?: string | null; // 'Pending', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'In Revision' date?: string | null; // Actual date completionDate?: string | null; // When completed estStartDate?: string | null; // Estimated start estCompletionDate?: string | null; // Estimated completion neededDate?: string | null; // Needed by detailLines?: string[]; // Additional detail events?: LicensingStageEvent[]; // History of events per phase isDelayed?: boolean; // Delay flag } ``` **Calendar Component:** - **Path:** `src/app/pages/calendar/calendar.component.ts` - Uses `angular-calendar` library (v0.29.0) - Integrated into itemDetail as tab (id='2') - Shows key dates for the item --- ## 7. Styling & Theming Conventions **Global Styles File:** `src/styles.scss` **Fonts Loaded (index.html):** - Material Icons: `https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons` - Material Symbols Outlined: `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined` - Inter (300, 400, 500, 600, 700): `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700` - Font Awesome 5.15.4: CDN link - PrimeIcons 7.0.0: CDN link **CSS Variables/Theming:** - Uses CSS custom properties for theming (referenced as `rgb(var(--color-primary))`) - Angular Material theme imported from `@vex/styles/core` - Tailwind CSS configured via `src/@vex/styles/tailwind.scss` **ItemDetail Component SCSS Variables (Custom):** ```scss .flex-field // flex: 1 1 calc(50% - 16px) — two-column layout .full-width // width: 100% .read-only-container // flex layout, centered, column direction .read-only-field // flex column for label/value pairs .read-only-label // font-weight: 600, color: #424242, font-size: 14px .read-only-value // font-size: 16px, color: #616161 .custom-tab-group // Custom border styling, uses CSS vars .design-only-overlay // Absolute positioned, rotated text overlay .info-icon // Blue (#5C77FF), 16x16px, inline-flex .mat-icon-sm // 16x20px for small Material Icons .dropdown-div // 2-column dropdown with code/description .custom-card // Border-radius 16px, shadow, hover transform .product-assignments-card // Grid layout with auto-fit minmax(190px) ``` **Color Scheme:** - Primary color: CSS var `--color-primary` (blue-ish, #1976d2 seen in links) - Background: White with subtle grays (#f7f9fc, #e2e6ea) - Text: Dark grays (#424242 labels, #616161 values) - Info icons: Bright blue (#5C77FF) **Responsive Breakpoints (styles.scss):** - `@media (min-width: 950px)` - Desktop layout with side-by-side image + cards - `@media (max-width: 950px)` - Mobile/tablet stacking **Global SCSS Mixins:** ```scss @mixin page-component-layout { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; } ``` --- ## 8. Icon Library **Icon System:** Material Icons (mat-icon) - Uses Google Material Icons font loaded in index.html - Also includes Material Symbols Outlined as fallback **Usage in ItemDetail:** ```html info close cancel ``` **No custom SVG icon set found** — relies entirely on Material Icons library. --- ## 9. Component Conventions & Architecture **Angular Version:** 21.1.3 (latest) **Component Type:** Traditional NgModule-based (not standalone) ```typescript // itemDetail.module.ts declares the component in an NgModule @NgModule({ declarations: [ItemDetailComponent, ...], imports: [MatCardModule, MatTabsModule, ...], providers: [TrackingService, AdminService, LicensingTrackingService] }) ``` **UI Library:** Angular Material 21.1.3 with: - `mat-card` for containers - `mat-tab-group` / `mat-tab` for tabbed sections - `mat-form-field` with `matInput` for text inputs - `mat-select` for dropdowns - `mat-datepicker` for date selection - `mat-chips` / `mat-chip-grid` for tags - `mat-icon` for icons - `mat-button` for buttons - FlexLayout (`@angular/flex-layout` v15.0.0-beta.42) for responsive layouts **Layout System:** - Uses `fxLayout="row wrap"` and `fxLayoutGap="16px"` for flex grids - Fallback CSS Grid in `.tracking` card (grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr)) - No bootstrap or custom CSS framework — pure Angular Material + Flex Layout **Data Binding:** - Two-way binding: `[(ngModel)]="itemData[0].field_name"` on inputs - Template interpolation: `{{ data.field_name | date }}` - Conditional rendering: `@if` and `@for` (Angular 17+ syntax) **Form Handling:** - Uses `FormsModule` ([(ngModel)]) - Uses `ReactiveFormsModule` for date controls with `[formControl]="dates[index]"` - No form-level validation visible in current code **Shared Components Imported:** - `` - Custom image uploader - `` - Comments/notes dialog - `` - Licensing tracker - `` - Calendar for key dates - `` - Progress tracker **Services:** - `ItemDetailService` - Item CRUD operations - `itemService` (main.service) - Global business data (MG codes, vendors, etc.) - `AdminService` - Admin operations - `LicensingTrackingService` - Licensing operations - `LoaderService` - Global loading indicator --- ## 10. Key Observations for Design Rebuild 1. **Data Complexity:** The item object has ~60+ fields spread across 3 content tabs + 2 async tabs (Licensing, Calendar). The design must accommodate all fields in an intuitive hierarchy. 2. **Division-Based Conditionals:** The UI changes significantly based on `div_code_fk` (line 9 = Spruce non-Lic shows different MG fields). Your design must account for this branching. 3. **Edit Mode Toggle:** The entire form switches between read-only and editable views. Both views must coexist in the template (currently uses `@if (!editing)` / `@else`). 4. **Nested Tabs:** Main content uses mat-tab-group with 3 tabs ("First", "Second", "Third"), then the outer component has async tabs (Main, Licensing, Calendar). The nesting is 2 levels deep. 5. **Field Names:** Use the exact `udf_*` and specific field names listed in Section 2. The "MG01-MG15" are conceptual labels; the real fields are `udf_merchgroup01`, `udf_merchgroup05_fk`, etc. 6. **MG Code Loading:** The backend returns a nested structure: `data[].merchGroupHeaders[].merchGroups[]`. The component loops through all and groups by `mgTypeDesc`. Your design doesn't need to know this; just bind to the dropdown options arrays. 7. **Flexbox + Grid Hybrid:** Current layout uses both `fxLayout` (flex-layout directive) for rows and CSS Grid for the tracking header. Use Material + Flex Layout for consistency. 8. **Responsive:** Two breakpoints at 950px width. Your redesign should maintain this or improve mobile UX. 9. **Tags as CSV:** Tags are stored as CSV string (`tags.join(',')`) and split on load. Component handles the edit chip-grid automatically. 10. **Licensing Status:** Fetched from a separate endpoint and displayed as a badge in the header. The licensing tab shows a stage-tracker component with phases. --- ## File Paths Summary | Purpose | Path | |---------|------| | Component | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.ts` | | Template | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.html` | | Styles | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.scss` | | Module | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.module.ts` | | Service | `src/app/helpers/services/item_detail.service.ts` | | Main Service | `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` | | Licensing Service | `src/app/helpers/services/licensing_tracking.service.ts` | | Licensing Tracker | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.ts` | | Calendar | `src/app/pages/calendar/calendar.component.ts` | | Global Styles | `src/styles.scss` | | Tailwind Config | `src/@vex/styles/tailwind.scss` | | Vex Theme | `src/@vex/styles/core` | --- You now have a complete map of the itemDetail page structure, all real field names, the MG code schema, routing, services, styling infrastructure, and UI conventions. This should provide everything needed to redesign the page comprehensively.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Explore the Angular app at D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend to map the existing **item detail** page so it can be redesigned. I need a thorough report. Investigate VERY thoroughly across multiple locations.

Find and report on:

1. **The itemDetail component**: Look under `src/app/pages/itemDetail/` (and anywhere similar like `item-detail`, `itemMaster`). Report the exact folder path and list its files (.ts, .html, .scss). For the component .ts file, quote: the @Component decorator (selector, templateUrl, styleUrls), the class properties (especially the item data model object and its field names), how it loads data (service calls, route params, ngOnInit), and the methods. Show me the real field names on the item object (e.g. itemNum, productType, etc.) so I can map the design's sample fields to real ones.

2. **The HTML template**: Summarize how it currently renders (~40 fields in flat rows across two sub-tabs per the design brief). Quote representative chunks showing the field bindings and the two sub-tab structure. Note what UI library is used (Angular Material? mat-tab? fxLayout? plain CSS grid?).

3. **Routing**: Find the route definition that maps to this component (e.g. `/apps/itemDetail/:id` or similar). Quote the route path and how the :id param is read.

4. **The data service & model**: Find the service that fetches the item and any TypeScript interface/model/class for the item. Quote the field names and types. I specifically need the real field names for: identity (item number, reference, art number, display name, description, division), merch-group codes (the MG01-MG15 fields — product type, sub-type, property, licensor, style guide, size, art source, artist, age group, for customer, etc.), specs (width, depth, weight, UOM, packaging, cost, lead time, vendor, note, season, product manager), status fields, dates, team/assignments, tags, attachments, comments.

5. **MG-code schema**: Search the codebase for "MG01", "MG02"... "MG15", "merchGroup", "merch_group", "mg_code" or similar. The design assumes MG01=productType, MG02=productSubType, MG03=productSubSubType, MG04=size, MG05=licensor, MG06=property, MG07=styleGuide, MG08=artSource, MG09=artist, MG10=ageGroup, MG15=forCustomer. Confirm or correct these against the real schema — report exactly what you find (field names, labels, any mapping table/config).

6. **Licensing & Calendar**: Find any existing licensing / license-tracking / license-stage-tracker component and any calendar/key-dates component related to items. Report paths and the data shape they consume.

7. **Styling/theming conventions**: How does this app do styling? SCSS with variables? A theme file? CSS custom properties already defined? Where is the global styles file? Is there an existing design-token/SCSS-variables file I should extend? What fonts are loaded (index.html / angular.json)? Report the global styles path and any existing color/spacing variables.

8. **Icon library**: What icons does the app use (mat-icon? a custom SVG set? font icons?). Report how icons are rendered so I can match the design's line-icon set.

9. **Component conventions**: standalone components vs NgModules? What Angular version (check package.json)? Are there shared/reusable UI components (cards, pills, badges) already? Where do shared components live?

Report concrete file paths (relative to designflow-frontend) and quote real field names and code. Do NOT modify anything. Be exhaustive — this drives a full page rebuild.

udf_date03 // Photography Recv Date udf_date05 // Fty Sent for Testing udf_date06 // Fty Sent for Photo created_time_date // Created time (index [5] in dates array) due_date // Due date (index [6]) // Additional Fields tags // CSV string of keyword tags (split/joined in component) udf_freeform_02 // Production status (displayed in header) udf_freeform_03 // Brand Assurance udf_freeform_05 // Cust Designed field udf_freeform_07 // Mold Paid Inv udf_freeform_08 // Contr Smpl PO udf_freeform_09 // Annu Smpl PO udf_item_priceA, B, C, D // Item prices (pricing tab) selling_price // Selling price retail_price // Retail price carton_length_size // Carton dimensions carton_width_size carton_depth_size carton_weight_size innerpack_qty // Inner pack quantity udf_int01, udf_int02 // UDF integer fields udfnum01 // UDF numeric field hts_num_ext_fk // HTS number royalty_code_fk // Royalty code mod_user_fk // Modified by user is_item_old // Flag: whether item is old (affects MG selection logic) productUserAssignments // Array of product team assignments // Images & Attachments itemAttachments // Array of file attachments ``` **Component Initialization (ngAfterViewInit/ngOnInit):** ```typescript async ngOnInit() { // 1. Get item ID from URL: /apps/itemDetail/:item this.itemDetailId = href[href.length - 1].split('?')[0] // 2. Load division code (calls getDivisionCode() which fetches itemData) const divisionCode = await this.getDivisionCode() // 3. Load MG codes grouped by type (Material, Construction, Feature, Size, Licensor, // Property, Big Theme, Little Theme, Style guide, Art Type, Art Source, Artist, Age Group) await this.itemService.getMerchGroup({ divisionCode }) // 4. Load item depth options, seasonal codes, company codes, vendors, UDF elements await this.getItemDepth() // 5. Call getItemInfo() to populate all dropdowns with current values await this.getItemInfo() // 6. Set up async tabs array (Main tab + Licensing (if not div 9) + Calendar) } ``` **Key Methods:** - `getDivisionCode()` - Fetches itemData from ItemDetailService via `getItemInfo({ id })` - `getItemInfo()` - Populates all selected* properties by matching item data to loaded options - `onSave()` - Marshals form values back to itemData object and calls `ItemDetailService.updateData()` - `onStyleNumber()` - Requests a new style number via `ItemDetailService.reqStyeNumber()` - `onProductAssignmentChange(role, event)` - Saves team member assignments - `loadProductAssignmentUsers()` - Fetches all users for product team dropdowns --- ## 3. HTML Template Structure **Layout Overview:** The template uses Angular Material (`mat-*`) components with `fxLayout` (FlexLayout) for responsive grid layouts. **Header Card (Top):** ```html Style #: {{data.item_num_id}} ``` **Main Tab Group (mat-tab-group with 3 nested content tabs):** **Tab 0 - "First" (Main Merchandising Tab):** - Read-only view: Displays ~18 fields in flex-wrap grid (2 columns per row) - Edit view: Same fields as mat-form-field inputs + editable tags - Fields shown: Active Status, Item Description, Product Type (MG01), Property (MG06), Product Sub-Type (MG02), Style Guide (MG07), Product Sub-Sub-Type (MG03), Item Content, Size (MG04), Brand Assurance, Season, Product Manager, Licensor (MG05), For Customer (MG15), Art Number reference **Tab 1 - "Second" (Specs & Dates Tab):** - Read-only view: Displays ~24 spec/date fields - Edit view: Inputs for dimensions, weights, dates (with mat-datepicker), vendor selection - Fields: Active Status, Discontinued Status, Item Width/Depth/Weight, UOMs, Item Note, Art Source (MG08), Packaging Type, Artist (MG09), Standard Cost, Age Group (MG10), Date fields (Earliest on Shelf, Due Date, Test Report, Photography, Testing/Photo Send), Mfg Lead Time, Vendor, Art Number **Tab 2 - "Third" (Pricing & Logistics Tab):** - Pricing: Item Prices A/B/C/D, Selling Price, Retail Price - Carton dimensions and weight, Inner pack qty - HTS Number, Royalty Code, Sales Person, Mold Paid Inv, various UDF fields, Created Time, Mod User **Two-Row Header Sub-Components (Right of main image):** 1. **Comments Card** - `` component 2. **Tech Pack Card** - Upload/View techpack files 3. **Tags Card** - Displays tags as chips (if not editing), or as editable chip-grid with ENTER to add 4. **Product Team Card** - 5 role dropdowns (Creative Designer, Creative Director, Technical Designer, Licensing, Salesperson) **Tabs 1+ (Conditional Tabs):** - **Licensing Tab** (tab.id='1'): Shows `` component (if div_code != 9) - **Calendar Tab** (tab.id='2'): Shows `` component with key dates **Edit/Save Buttons:** - Read-only: "Edit" button + "Request a style number" / "Update Item Details" button - Edit mode: "Save" and "Close" buttons --- ## 4. Data Service & Model **Service File:** `src/app/helpers/services/item_detail.service.ts` **Key Methods:** ```typescript getItemInfo(id: any) // POST /detail/getItemInfo updateData(data: any) // POST /detail/updateData reqStyeNumber(data: any) // POST /detail/reqStyeNumber getComments(id: number) // GET /detail/getComments/:id addComment(comment: CommentInterface) // POST /detail/addComment UpdateLicensingStatus(data) // POST /detail/UpdateLicensingStatus GetLicensingStatus(id) // GET /detail/GetLicensingStatus/:id getProductAssignments(itemId) // GET /detail/assignments/:itemId setProductAssignments(itemId, assignments) // POST /detail/assignments/:itemId ``` **Type Definitions:** ```typescript export type ProductAssignmentRole = | 'creative_designer' | 'creative_director' | 'technical_designer' | 'licensing_person' | 'salesperson'; export interface ProductAssignmentUser { id: number; name: string; lastname: string; email: string; profile_photo?: string | null; level?: string; } export interface ProductAssignment { id: number; item_id_fk: number; role: ProductAssignmentRole; user_id_fk: number; user?: ProductAssignmentUser; } ``` **Main Service:** `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` **Key Methods for ItemDetail:** ```typescript getMerchGroup(data: { divisionCode }) // POST /getMerchGroup getSeasonCode() // GET /getSeasonCode getCompanyCode() // GET /getCompanyCode getAllVendors() // GET /getAllVendors getItemDepth() // GET /findItemDepth getCustomers() // GET /findCustomers getUDFElements(data) // POST /getUDFElement ``` --- ## 5. MG-Code (Merchandise Group) Schema **Real Schema Mapping (NOT as assumed in design brief):** The actual code loads MG codes from the backend grouped by **mgTypeDesc** (not by numeric code). The component maps them to the following logical purposes: | Display Label | Field Name | Backend mgTypeDesc | Component Property | Notes | |---|---|---|---|---| | Product Type | udf_merchgroup01 | "Material" | selectedProductType | MG01 logically | | Product Sub-Type | udf_merchgroup02 | "Construction" | selectedProductSubType | MG02 logically | | Product Sub-Sub-Type | udf_merchgroup03 | "Feature" | selectedProductSubSubType | MG03 logically | | Size | udf_merchgroup04 | "Size" | selectedSize | MG04 logically | | Licensor (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup05_fk | "Licensor" | selectedLicensor | MG05 logically | | Big Theme (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup05_fk | "Big Theme" | selectedBigTheme | MG05 alternative for div=9 | | Property (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup06_fk | "Property" | selectedProperty | MG06 logically | | Little Theme (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup06_fk | "Little Theme" | selectedLittleTheme | MG06 alternative for div=9 | | Style Guide (non-Spruce9) | udf_merchgroup07_fk | "Style guide" | selectedStyleGuide | MG07 logically | | Art Type (Spruce9 only) | udf_merchgroup07_fk | "Art Type" | selectedArtType | MG07 alternative for div=9 | | Art Source | udf_merchgroup08_fk | "Art Source" | selectedArtSource | MG08 logically | | Artist | udf_merchgroup09_fk | "Artist" | selectedArtist | MG09 logically | | Age Group | udf_merchgroup10_fk | "Age Group" | selectedAgeGroup | MG10 logically | | For Customer | udf_merchgroup15_fk | (custom/customer table) | selectedForCustomer | MG15 logically | **Database Field Structure for Each MG:** ```typescript { mg_id: number, // Primary key mg_code: string, // Short code (used in dropdowns) mgCode2: string, // Alternative code (displayed in dropdowns) mg_desc: string, // Full description/title is_active: boolean, // Active flag parent_id?: number, // Parent category ID divisionCode_id_fk: number, // Division FK (for filtering by division) createdTime: Date, // Created timestamp (year extracted for old-item logic) mgTypeDesc: string // Type descriptor (Material, Construction, etc.) } ``` **Division-Specific Behavior:** - **Division 9 (Spruce non-Lic)**: Uses Big Theme, Little Theme, Art Type fields instead of Licensor, Property, Style Guide - **Other divisions**: Use standard Licensor, Property, Style Guide fields **Old Item Logic (lines 500-507):** - If `is_item_old === true`, MG selection matches by `mg_code` instead of `id` - Filters to inactive items NOT created in 2025 - Allows showing historical MG codes no longer in the active list --- ## 6. Licensing & Calendar Components **Licensing Component:** - **Path:** `src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/` - **Files:** - `license-tracking.component.ts` - `licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.ts` - Tracks phases (Submission, Resubmission, PPS Approval) - `licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.html` - `licensing_tracking_button/licensing_tracking_button.component.ts` - `feedback_dialog/feedback_dialog.component.ts` **Licensing Stage Tracker Data Shape:** ```typescript interface LicensingStageRow { phase: string; // 'Submission', 'Resubmission', 'PPS Approval' status?: string | null; // 'Pending', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'In Revision' date?: string | null; // Actual date completionDate?: string | null; // When completed estStartDate?: string | null; // Estimated start estCompletionDate?: string | null; // Estimated completion neededDate?: string | null; // Needed by detailLines?: string[]; // Additional detail events?: LicensingStageEvent[]; // History of events per phase isDelayed?: boolean; // Delay flag } ``` **Calendar Component:** - **Path:** `src/app/pages/calendar/calendar.component.ts` - Uses `angular-calendar` library (v0.29.0) - Integrated into itemDetail as tab (id='2') - Shows key dates for the item --- ## 7. Styling & Theming Conventions **Global Styles File:** `src/styles.scss` **Fonts Loaded (index.html):** - Material Icons: `https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons` - Material Symbols Outlined: `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined` - Inter (300, 400, 500, 600, 700): `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700` - Font Awesome 5.15.4: CDN link - PrimeIcons 7.0.0: CDN link **CSS Variables/Theming:** - Uses CSS custom properties for theming (referenced as `rgb(var(--color-primary))`) - Angular Material theme imported from `@vex/styles/core` - Tailwind CSS configured via `src/@vex/styles/tailwind.scss` **ItemDetail Component SCSS Variables (Custom):** ```scss .flex-field // flex: 1 1 calc(50% - 16px) — two-column layout .full-width // width: 100% .read-only-container // flex layout, centered, column direction .read-only-field // flex column for label/value pairs .read-only-label // font-weight: 600, color: #424242, font-size: 14px .read-only-value // font-size: 16px, color: #616161 .custom-tab-group // Custom border styling, uses CSS vars .design-only-overlay // Absolute positioned, rotated text overlay .info-icon // Blue (#5C77FF), 16x16px, inline-flex .mat-icon-sm // 16x20px for small Material Icons .dropdown-div // 2-column dropdown with code/description .custom-card // Border-radius 16px, shadow, hover transform .product-assignments-card // Grid layout with auto-fit minmax(190px) ``` **Color Scheme:** - Primary color: CSS var `--color-primary` (blue-ish, #1976d2 seen in links) - Background: White with subtle grays (#f7f9fc, #e2e6ea) - Text: Dark grays (#424242 labels, #616161 values) - Info icons: Bright blue (#5C77FF) **Responsive Breakpoints (styles.scss):** - `@media (min-width: 950px)` - Desktop layout with side-by-side image + cards - `@media (max-width: 950px)` - Mobile/tablet stacking **Global SCSS Mixins:** ```scss @mixin page-component-layout { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; } ``` --- ## 8. Icon Library **Icon System:** Material Icons (mat-icon) - Uses Google Material Icons font loaded in index.html - Also includes Material Symbols Outlined as fallback **Usage in ItemDetail:** ```html info close cancel ``` **No custom SVG icon set found** — relies entirely on Material Icons library. --- ## 9. Component Conventions & Architecture **Angular Version:** 21.1.3 (latest) **Component Type:** Traditional NgModule-based (not standalone) ```typescript // itemDetail.module.ts declares the component in an NgModule @NgModule({ declarations: [ItemDetailComponent, ...], imports: [MatCardModule, MatTabsModule, ...], providers: [TrackingService, AdminService, LicensingTrackingService] }) ``` **UI Library:** Angular Material 21.1.3 with: - `mat-card` for containers - `mat-tab-group` / `mat-tab` for tabbed sections - `mat-form-field` with `matInput` for text inputs - `mat-select` for dropdowns - `mat-datepicker` for date selection - `mat-chips` / `mat-chip-grid` for tags - `mat-icon` for icons - `mat-button` for buttons - FlexLayout (`@angular/flex-layout` v15.0.0-beta.42) for responsive layouts **Layout System:** - Uses `fxLayout="row wrap"` and `fxLayoutGap="16px"` for flex grids - Fallback CSS Grid in `.tracking` card (grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr)) - No bootstrap or custom CSS framework — pure Angular Material + Flex Layout **Data Binding:** - Two-way binding: `[(ngModel)]="itemData[0].field_name"` on inputs - Template interpolation: `{{ data.field_name | date }}` - Conditional rendering: `@if` and `@for` (Angular 17+ syntax) **Form Handling:** - Uses `FormsModule` ([(ngModel)]) - Uses `ReactiveFormsModule` for date controls with `[formControl]="dates[index]"` - No form-level validation visible in current code **Shared Components Imported:** - `` - Custom image uploader - `` - Comments/notes dialog - `` - Licensing tracker - `` - Calendar for key dates - `` - Progress tracker **Services:** - `ItemDetailService` - Item CRUD operations - `itemService` (main.service) - Global business data (MG codes, vendors, etc.) - `AdminService` - Admin operations - `LicensingTrackingService` - Licensing operations - `LoaderService` - Global loading indicator --- ## 10. Key Observations for Design Rebuild 1. **Data Complexity:** The item object has ~60+ fields spread across 3 content tabs + 2 async tabs (Licensing, Calendar). The design must accommodate all fields in an intuitive hierarchy. 2. **Division-Based Conditionals:** The UI changes significantly based on `div_code_fk` (line 9 = Spruce non-Lic shows different MG fields). Your design must account for this branching. 3. **Edit Mode Toggle:** The entire form switches between read-only and editable views. Both views must coexist in the template (currently uses `@if (!editing)` / `@else`). 4. **Nested Tabs:** Main content uses mat-tab-group with 3 tabs ("First", "Second", "Third"), then the outer component has async tabs (Main, Licensing, Calendar). The nesting is 2 levels deep. 5. **Field Names:** Use the exact `udf_*` and specific field names listed in Section 2. The "MG01-MG15" are conceptual labels; the real fields are `udf_merchgroup01`, `udf_merchgroup05_fk`, etc. 6. **MG Code Loading:** The backend returns a nested structure: `data[].merchGroupHeaders[].merchGroups[]`. The component loops through all and groups by `mgTypeDesc`. Your design doesn't need to know this; just bind to the dropdown options arrays. 7. **Flexbox + Grid Hybrid:** Current layout uses both `fxLayout` (flex-layout directive) for rows and CSS Grid for the tracking header. Use Material + Flex Layout for consistency. 8. **Responsive:** Two breakpoints at 950px width. Your redesign should maintain this or improve mobile UX. 9. **Tags as CSV:** Tags are stored as CSV string (`tags.join(',')`) and split on load. Component handles the edit chip-grid automatically. 10. **Licensing Status:** Fetched from a separate endpoint and displayed as a badge in the header. The licensing tab shows a stage-tracker component with phases. --- ## File Paths Summary | Purpose | Path | |---------|------| | Component | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.ts` | | Template | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.html` | | Styles | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.scss` | | Module | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.module.ts` | | Service | `src/app/helpers/services/item_detail.service.ts` | | Main Service | `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` | | Licensing Service | `src/app/helpers/services/licensing_tracking.service.ts` | | Licensing Tracker | `src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/licensing-stage-tracker/licensing-stage-tracker.component.ts` | | Calendar | `src/app/pages/calendar/calendar.component.ts` | | Global Styles | `src/styles.scss` | | Tailwind Config | `src/@vex/styles/tailwind.scss` | | Vex Theme | `src/@vex/styles/core` | --- You now have a complete map of the itemDetail page structure, all real field names, the MG code schema, routing, services, styling infrastructure, and UI conventions. This should provide everything needed to redesign the page comprehensively.

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code notes for designflow-frontend

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first. It is the canonical guide for repo structure, custom-code boundaries, current architecture, incidents, deployment, and pending work. This file is only for Claude Code-specific operating notes.

## Memory and Branch

- User memory file: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Commit and push only to `sandbox-albert`
- Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, push that updated `sandbox-albert` to GitHub, then pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally before editing.
- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; use `develop` only for sync/PR base. Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`.
- Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then keep the GitHub PR `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated, or create it if missing.

## Ignore Guidance

Use `.claudeignore`. It matches `.cursorignore` and excludes generated, vendored, cache, asset, and legacy/demo template paths such as `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.angular/`, `.cache/`, `.yarn/`, `coverage/`, `src/assets/`, `src/static-data/`, and Vex demo pages.

## Allowed Operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, run `yarn`/`npm` scripts, run `ng`, use `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push origin sandbox-albert`
- Not allowed by default: force-push, rewrite history, edit `node_modules/` or `dist/`
- SSH is not the normal deployment path. Deployment is Cloud Build -> Docker -> Cloud Run after branch push; use `gcloud` only for explicit rollback or incident triage.

## Tests

```powershell
yarn test
yarn test --testPathPatterns="rfq.grid.config"
yarn test:watch
```

Run the relevant tests before committing. Add or update unit tests for every new or changed function/behavior. The Docker build also runs `yarn test`.

## Claude-Specific Rules

- Use the AG Grid MCP docs before touching AG Grid code: set version `35.1.0`, framework `angular`; the repo uses AG Grid `35.2.1`.
- Do not use `.toPromise()` in new code; use `firstValueFrom()`.
- Do not add new `window['anything']` globals; use Angular DI.
- Do not add `aggFunc` to RFQ grid columns without reading `AGENTS.md`.
- For `mat-select`, keep options in a synchronous array, do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]`, and never add `(click)="select.open()"`.

## Commit Style

```text
type(scope): short description
```

Use `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, or `chore`. Keep messages specific.

content.content: Updated task #2 status

content.text: Now the SCSS — porting all tokens and the design-system classes. Appending to the component stylesheet, scoped under `.df-redesign`.

content.content: The file D:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\itemDetail\itemDetail.component.scss has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +   Item Detail — Direction A "Two-column dossier" redesign
+   Design tokens + design-system classes (ported from the handoff spec).
+   All scoped under .df-redesign so the rest of the app is untouched.
+/* ---- hue palette (badge / field bar / labels / chips / tabs) ---- */
+  &.df-pill--brand   { background: var(--brand-50); color: var(--brand-700); .df-dot { background: var(--brand); } }
+  &.df-pill--accent  { background: var(--accent-50); color: var(--accent-700); .df-dot { background: var(--accent); } }
+  .df-header-main { min-width: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; }
+  .df-header-status { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap; }
+  .df-header-actions { display: flex; gap: 9px; flex: none; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: flex-end; }
+  &.df-btn--primary { background: var(--brand); color: #fff; border-color: var(--brand); &:hover { background: var(--brand-700); border-color: var(--brand-700); } }
+  &.df-btn--accent  { background: var(--accent); color: #fff; border-color: var(--accent); &:hover { background: var(--accent-700); border-color: var(--accent-700); } }
+  &.df-btn--outline { background: var(--paper); color: var(--ink); border-color: var(--line); &:hover { background: var(--chip); } }
+  &.df-btn--ghost   { background: transparent; color: var(--ink-2); &:hover { background: var(--chip); } }
+  .df-card-head-l { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; min-width: 0; }
+  .df-band-id { flex: none; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; }
+  .df-band-sep { width: 1px; height: 30px; background: var(--line-2); flex: none; }
+  .df-band-facts { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 22px; min-width: 0; }
+  .df-fact-k { font-size: 9.5px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink-3); }
+    .mat-icon, mat-icon { width: 9px; height: 9px; font-size: 9px; line-height: 9px; color: #fff; }
+  .df-step-date { margin-top: 2px; font-size: 10.5px; color: var(--ink-3); font-family: var(--mono); }
+    &.is-active { background: var(--paper); color: var(--ink); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(28, 27, 34, 0.12); }
+  .df-team-name { font-size: 12.5px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--ink); white-space: nowrap; }
+    display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
+  .df-file-name { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--ink); overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }
+  &.is-first { background: var(--brand-50); color: var(--brand-700); border-color: transparent; }
+/* gallery / image card: let the existing image-upload fill it nicely */
+/* keep the edit-mode forms readable inside the redesigned shell */
+.df-redesign .df-edit-shell { background: var(--paper); border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 12px; padding: 8px 4px; }

content.text:
The existing spec `itemDetail-for-cust-placement.spec.ts` encodes a real requirement: **For Cust** must appear right after **Cust Designed** in the First set (and not in Second). My redesign dropped the "Cust Designed" field and changed the markup, which would break that requirement. I'll re-add **Cust Designed** (the design's field list is explicitly "illustrative") and update the spec to the new structure while preserving its intent.

content.text:
Done. The Item Detail page now renders **Direction A** (the selected design), wired to live data, and the app builds cleanly.

## What I built

**Direction A · Main tab**, recreated in the real Angular + Material environment (not the prototype's inline styles):

- **Page header** — `Active`/division status pills, the item display name as `h1`, and a mono identity row (Style # / Reference # / Art #, with Art # linking to the art-piece route). Actions: *Edit Details* (enters the existing edit flow) + the existing `Request a style number` / `Update Item Details` (ColdLion) button.
- **Colored primary tab bar** — custom Main / Licensing / Calendar tabs with tinted icon badges (replaces the plain `mat-tab-group`, driven by `selectedTabIndex`, div-9 still hides Licensing, `?tab=licensing` deep-link preserved).
- **Production timeline band** — a 5-milestone stepper (Created → Test Report → Photography → Due → On Shelf) whose done/current/todo state is computed from the item's real date fields, plus the accent "Due" pill.
- **Gallery + At a glance** — the real `<image-upload>` (with the Design-Only overlay) beside an amber summary card.
- **Item Attributes** — the 4-column **colored field grid** with the `First / Second / Third` segmented control. Each field carries its hue, left bar, and mono MG-code chip.
- **Bottom row** — Product Team (read-only avatar roster ↔ edit selects), Tech Pack & Files, Tags, and the existing Comments (`view-notes-dialog`).

**Preserved intact:** edit mode (all First/Second/Third forms, save/close), `onSave`, product-team assignment saves, tags chip editor, image upload, techpack dialogs, and the Licensing (`license-tracking`) and Calendar (`vex-calendar`) tabs — placed inside the new card shell.

**Tokens:** all colors / 8-hue palette / pill tones / type scale / spacing ported to `itemDetail.component.scss` (scoped under `.df-redesign`), Hanken Grotesk + IBM Plex Mono added to `index.html`, responsive collapse at 1180/720px.

## On your MG-code flag
I confirmed the MG → field mapping against the real schema (`getMerchGroup`, grouped by `mgTypeDesc`): MG01=Product Type, MG02=Sub-Type, MG03=Sub-Sub-Type, MG04=Size, MG05=Licensor, MG06=Property, MG07=Style Guide, MG08=Art Source, MG09=Artist, MG10=Age Group, MG15=For Customer — **all match the docs**. The one real-schema subtlety the docs didn't cover: for **division 9 (Spruce non-Lic)**, MG05/06/07 become **Big Theme / Little Theme / Art Type** — I wired both variants. I also re-added the **Cust Designed** field (the design's list was "illustrative" and an existing spec requires it directly above For Cust).

## Verification
- `ng build --configuration sandbox` → **exit 0, 0 errors, 0 warnings** from my SCSS.
- `jest` itemDetail specs → **11/11 pass** (updated the For-Cust-placement spec to the new markup + added a spec for the new timeline/initials/team/division helpers).

Files: [itemDetail.component.html](designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.html), [itemDetail.component.ts](designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.ts), [itemDetail.component.scss](designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.scss), [index.html](designflow-frontend/src/index.html), plus the two specs.

## Notes / follow-ups
- The design's bespoke **Licensing** (StageTracker/FeedbackTable) and **Calendar** (MiniCalendar/agenda) tab layouts are **not** applied — they're separate existing components, and the README sequences them after A·Main. That's the natural next step if you want them.
- To see it live you'll need the app running against a real item (`/apps/itemDetail/:id`). Want me to start the dev server, or **commit to `sandbox-albert`** (and update the PR to `develop`) per your usual flow?

content.content:
{"body":"## RFQ page polish (design handoff)

Implements four approved UX/visual improvements on the RFQ module, one PR-sized commit each. Full RFQ spec suite green (96 tests across `rfq.component.spec`, `rfq.grid.config.spec`, `rfq.detail.grid.config.spec`) and `tsc --noEmit` clean.

1. **Detail sub-grid flex sizing** (`rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` + live `detail-cell-renderer`) — Note is the sole flexing column (`flex:1` + `minWidth`, no hard width); priced/lookup columns get minWidth floors + right-alignment; Price next to Name. Gapless at any width; no `sizeColumnsToFit` alongside flex.
2. **Density toggle** (`rfq.component.ts`/`.html`) — Compact/Standard/Comfortable row+header heights via `setGridOption` + `resetRowHeights`, persisted to `localStorage['rfq:density']`, restored on grid ready. 3-segment `mat-button-toggle` in the toolbar.
3. **Frozen identity + seam** (`rfq.grid.config.ts` + RFQ scss) — `pic`/`desc` pinned left (design widths 56/280); seam (rule + shadow) on the last pinned-left header/cell; brand pink-soft active-row highlight spanning the seam.
4. **Collapsible pricing scenario column groups** (`rfq.component.ts` + scss) — Gen/Lic × FOB/POE/WHSE/MDDP leaves wrapped into AG-Grid column groups (Price-for-Sales always visible; Sell/Margin/Royalty `columnGroupShow:'open'`; `marryChildren`); toolbar expand/collapse-all via `setColumnGroupOpened`; group-band header tints.

### Constraints honored
- All row-data access stays on `getMasterFiledName()`; `this.colHeaders` stays flat — grouping is layered onto `columnDefs` only.
- Did not touch the fill-handle double-click blocker, native undo, or the detail default price sort.
- No column has both `flex` and `sizeColumnsToFit`.

### Notes / follow-ups
- `groupDefaultExpanded` intentionally left as-is (that controls *row* grouping; column-group collapse is handled by `columnGroupShow`).
- Column-group open/closed state is session-only; persisting it in named-view layouts (`layout-dropdown`) is a deliberate follow-up.
- Verified via tests + typecheck; not yet eyeballed in a running browser.

---

## (Earlier in this branch) poppim PM integration analysis + AG Grid 35.3 deps gotcha

Documentation-only. Records durable knowledge from a session that (a) analyzed how DesignFlow's `licensing_tracking` module overlaps with the **poppim** PM system, and (b) hit a phantom test failure worth flagging for future sessions.

- **`docs/licensing-tracking-poppim-integration.md` (new)** — `licensing_tracking` vs poppim overlap; separate clouds/DBs, no existing integration; chosen direction: DesignFlow stays system of record, poppim consumes a read-only copy; shared key is the DesignFlow `item_num_id` embedded in poppim's `product.name`. 9,293 distinct item-nos across 58% of 16,534 poppim rows.
- **`AGENTS.md`** — §14 incident-log entry: phantom `rfq.component.spec` TS errors were stale `node_modules` after a `develop` merge bumped AG Grid 35.2.1 → 35.3; fix is `yarn install`.
- **`HANDOFF.md`** — shared-key verification handoff (blocked on a read-only DB export), with the exact A/B/C queries and next action.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","title":"RFQ page polish (detail flex, density, frozen identity, collapsible scenarios) + poppim docs"}

cd /d/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend; cat > /tmp/itemdetail_section.md <<'EOF'
## Item Detail page redesign — Direction A ("two-column dossier")

Recreates the approved redesign of the **item detail** Main tab (`/apps/itemDetail/:id`) in the real Angular + Material environment (the prototype's inline styles were treated as a value spec, not shipped). Build green (`ng build --configuration sandbox`, 0 errors); itemDetail jest specs **11/11**.

- **Page header** — Active/division status pills, display name `h1`, mono identity row (Style # / Reference # / Art # → art-piece link); actions: *Edit Details* (existing edit flow) + the existing ColdLion `Request a style number` / `Update Item Details`.
- **Colored primary tab bar** — custom Main/Licensing/Calendar tabs with tinted icon badges (replaces the plain `mat-tab-group`; div-9 still hides Licensing; `?tab=licensing` deep-link preserved).
- **Production timeline band** — 5-milestone stepper with done/current/todo state computed from the item's real date fields + accent "Due" pill.
- **Gallery + At a glance**, **Item Attributes** 4-column colored field grid with a First/Second/Third segmented control (MG-code chips), and a **Product Team / Tech Pack / Tags / Comments** bottom row.
- **Tokens** ported to scoped SCSS (`.df-redesign`): colors, 8-hue palette, pill tones, type scale, spacing; Hanken Grotesk + IBM Plex Mono added to `index.html`; responsive collapse at 1180/720px.

### Data wiring & constraints honored
- Bound to real fields (`item_displ_descr_name`, `item_num_id`, `ref_num`, `dsn_ref_num`, `udf_merchgroup01-15`, dates, vendor, assignments). MG01-MG15 mapping confirmed against the real `getMerchGroup`/`mgTypeDesc` schema; **division-9** Big Theme / Little Theme / Art Type variant wired.
- Edit mode, product-team assignment saves, image upload, techpack dialogs, tags editor, comments, and the Licensing (`license-tracking`) / Calendar (`vex-calendar`) tabs preserved.
- Re-added the **Cust Designed** field above For Cust; updated `itemDetail-for-cust-placement.spec` to the new markup and added `itemDetail-redesign-helpers.spec` (timeline/initials/team/division getters).

### Notes / follow-ups
- The design's bespoke **Licensing** (StageTracker/FeedbackTable) and **Calendar** (MiniCalendar/agenda) tab layouts are not applied — those are separate existing components and the handoff sequences them after A·Main.
- Not yet eyeballed against a running item in the browser.

---

EOF

Confirmed the diagnosis:
- `getItemTypes` (sibling `/lib` route) → **403** (route exists, auth blocks) — the deployed service and `/lib` mount are fine.
- `getGridCellNotes` + `setGridCellNote` → **404** — these routes are **missing on the deployed item-master service**.

So the running item-master Cloud Run service on alsand predates the cell-notes commit (`aead588 "extened notes to other modules"`), even though `origin/sandbox-albert` has it. My frontend deploy just surfaced the gap. The fix is to redeploy item-master. Let me check its deploy setup and recent build history.

file.content:
steps:
  # Build the image using Dockerfile
  - id: Build the image
    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    args:
      - build
      - -t
      - ${_IMAGE}
      - --build-arg=NODE_ENV=${_APP_ENV}
      - .

  # Push the built image to artifact registry
  - id: Push the image to artifact registry
    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    args:
      - push
      - ${_IMAGE}

  # Deploy container image to Cloud Run
  - id: Deploy the image to cloud run
    name: 'gcr.io/google.com/cloudsdktool/cloud-sdk'
    entrypoint: gcloud
    args: 
      - run
      - deploy
      - ${_SERVICE_NAME}
      - --image=${_IMAGE}
      - --region=${_REGION}
      - --port=${_PORT}
      - --concurrency=${_CONCURRENCY}
      - --max-instances=${_MAX_INSTANCES}
      - --vpc-connector=${_VPC_CONNECTOR}
      - --no-allow-unauthenticated
      - --platform=${_PLATFORM}
      - --service-account=${_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME}
      - --memory=${_MEMORY}
      - |-
        --set-secrets=
        DB_USER=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/DB_USER/versions/latest,
        DB_PASSWORD=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/DB_PASSWORD/versions/latest,
        DB_NAME=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/DB_NAME/versions/latest,
        DB_HOST=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/DB_HOST/versions/latest,
        DB_PORT=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/DB_PORT/versions/latest,
        TEAMS_CLIENT_SECRET=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/TEAMS_CLIENT_SECRET/versions/latest,
        TEAMS_TENANT_ID=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/TEAMS_TENANT_ID/versions/latest,
        TEAMS_CLIENT_ID=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/TEAMS_CLIENT_ID/versions/latest,
        JWT_PRIVATE_KEY=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/JWT_PRIVATE_KEY/versions/latest,
        JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/JWT_PUBLIC_KEY/versions/latest,
        COLDLION_API_KEY=projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/COLDLION_API_KEY/versions/latest,
      - |-
        --set-env-vars=
        WEB_URL=${_WEB_URL},
        API_ADDRESS=${_API_ADDRESS},
        SCHEMA=${_DB_SCHEMA}

substitutions:
  _REGION: $$_REGION
  _SERVICE_NAME: $$_SERVICE_NAME
  _APP_ENV: $$_APP_ENV
  _APP_NAME: $$_APP_NAME
  _SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME: $$_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME
  _PORT: $$_PORT
  _CONCURRENCY: $$_CONCURRENCY
  _MAX_INSTANCES: $$_MAX_INSTANCES
  _VPC_CONNECTOR: $$_VPC_CONNECTOR
  _PLATFORM: $$_PLATFORM
  _MEMORY: $$_MEMORY
  _ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME: $$_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME
  _WEB_URL: $$_WEB_URL
  _API_ADDRESS: $$_API_ADDRESS
  _DB_SCHEMA: $$_DB_SCHEMA
  _IMAGE: ${_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${PROJECT_ID}/${_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/${_APP_ENV}/${_APP_NAME}:${BUILD_ID}-${COMMIT_SHA}

options:
  logging: CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY

images:
  - ${_IMAGE}

content.text: Important finding: the most recent build in this project is the **frontend** on 06-12 — there's **no recent item-master sandbox build at all** (only 2024 bitbucket ones). So the item-master service on alsand was never redeployed after the cell-notes route landed (06-13). The fix is to run the item-master sandbox deploy. Let me find its Cloud Build trigger.

input.command: gcloud builds triggers list --format="value(name, github.name, github.push.branch, substitutions._SERVICE_NAME)" 2>&1 | grep -iE "item|master|albert|sand" | head -30; echo "=== all trigger names ==="; gcloud builds triggers list --format="value(name)" 2>&1 | head -40

content.text: No Cloud Build *triggers* exist here — and that 06-12 frontend build has blank repo/branch, meaning deploys are done **manually via `gcloud builds submit`**, not auto-triggered on push. That's why neither my frontend push nor the 06-13 item-master commit auto-deployed. Let me find the item-master Cloud Run service and the exact substitutions used, so a redeploy matches.

input.command: echo "=== Cloud Run services ==="; gcloud run services list --format="value(metadata.name, region, status.url)" 2>&1 | grep -iE "item|albert|sand|master" | head; echo "=== full list (names) ==="; gcloud run services list --format="value(metadata.name, region)" 2>&1 | head -40

content.text:
**Root cause found.** Revision 00012's container start command is:

```
$ npm test && cross-env NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert nodemon index.js
```

The startup runs **`npm test` (jest) before starting the server**. The tests pass, but jest prints *"Jest did not exit one second after the test run has completed"* — a test added with the cell-notes feature leaves an **open handle**, so jest never exits → `&& nodemon index.js` never runs → the server never listens on PORT 5003 → health check fails → Cloud Run keeps traffic on the old revision 00011 (which lacks the route). Revision 00011 (06-11) predates that hanging test, which is why it deployed fine.

So the real fix is in the item-master repo: the container must not gate server startup on the test run. Let me inspect the start script and Dockerfile.

input.command: cd /d/repos/dflow/designflow-item-master; echo "=== package.json scripts ==="; node -e "console.log(JSON.stringify(require('./package.json').scripts,null,1))"; echo "=== Dockerfile CMD/ENTRYPOINT ==="; grep -niE "cmd|entrypoint|npm|yarn|start" Dockerfile 2>&1

content.text: This is the real bug — and it's reversible (if a new revision fails, Cloud Run just keeps serving 00011, same as now). The container start command gates the server on `npm test`, and a test added with the cell-notes feature leaves an open handle so jest never exits. The fix: make jest exit. Let me see the full Dockerfile to plan the rebuild.

ARG NODE_ENV
ENV NODE_ENV $NODE_ENV
RUN apk add --update imagemagick
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN mkdir -p .yarn-cache && yarn --cache-folder .yarn-cache --production=false
EXPOSE 5000
CMD yarn start:$NODE_ENV

content.text: The fix: add `--forceExit` to the `test` script so jest always terminates (it passes, it just hangs on an open handle), letting the `npm test &&` startup gate complete and the server start. This also hardens all four `start:*` scripts against future hanging-handle regressions.

content.text: Tests pass in the **container** (14/14 per the deploy logs), but locally 2 tests in `item-number-generation.test.js` fail at a `Math.random` spy `mockRestore()` — and the local total differs (13 vs 14). That smells like local env/jest-version flakiness, but it also reveals the deeper fragility: **gating container startup on the test suite means any flaky/failing test takes the server down** — exactly this outage. Let me see the failing test to judge whether it's env-only before deciding how aggressively to fix.

In the container, `yarn --production=false` installs everything, so all 14 tests pass (as the deploy log confirmed). So `--forceExit` is exactly the right fix: tests pass in-container, and now jest will exit instead of hanging, so the server starts. Let me commit, then rebuild + redeploy.

content.text: No ignore files for Docker, but `gcloud builds submit` respects `.gitignore` by default. Let me confirm `node_modules` is gitignored (so the upload stays clean like the original trigger builds), then commit and push the fix.

input.command:
cd /d/repos/dflow/designflow-item-master; echo "=== node_modules ignored? ==="; git check-ignore node_modules && echo "YES (gcloud will skip it)" || echo "NO"; echo "=== commit+push fix ==="; git commit -m "fix(startup): force jest to exit so the server boots in the container

The container CMD is 'yarn start:\$NODE_ENV', and every start:* script runs
'npm test && ... nodemon index.js'. After the grid-cell-notes tests were added,
jest leaves an open handle and never exits, so the '&&' gate never proceeds and
the server never listens on PORT 5003. Cloud Run's startup probe then fails and
traffic stays pinned to the previous revision (which lacks the /lib/getGridCellNotes
route), surfacing as a 404 toast on the item library page.

Add --forceExit to the jest script so the (passing) test run terminates and the
server starts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push origin sandbox-albert 2>&1 | tail -4

input.command:
cd /d/repos/dflow/designflow-item-master; cat > /tmp/im_section.md <<'EOF'
## Fix: item-master container failed to boot → item library 404 (getGridCellNotes)

The container CMD is `yarn start:$NODE_ENV`, and every `start:*` script runs
`npm test && … nodemon index.js`. After the grid-cell-notes tests were added,
jest left an open handle and never exited, so the `&&` gate never reached the
server — the container never listened on `PORT=5003`. Cloud Run's startup probe
failed (revision `…-00012` = `HealthCheckContainerError`) and traffic stayed
pinned to the previous healthy revision, which predates the
`/lib/getGridCellNotes` + `/lib/setGridCellNote` routes. Result: the item
library page toasted `HTTP 404 … /api/item_master/lib/getGridCellNotes`.

- **`package.json`** — `test` → `jest --forceExit`, so the (passing) startup test
  run always terminates and the server boots. Verified live: those routes now
  return 403 (auth) instead of 404; new revision healthy, 100% traffic.
- **`.gcloudignore` (new)** — excludes `node_modules`/artifacts from
  `gcloud builds submit` uploads (node_modules isn't gitignored here).

Deployed to `popcre-albert-item-sandbox` (us-central1) via a manual build + image
deploy.

---

EOF
gh pr view 22 --json body -q .body > /tmp/im_body_old.md
cat /tmp/im_section.md /tmp/im_body_old.md > /tmp/im_body_new.md
gh pr edit 22 --title "Fix item-master container boot (item library 404) + bound item-number collision retries" --body-file /tmp/im_body_new.md 2>&1 | tail -2; echo "EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}"

input.content: --- name: project-sandbox-deploy description: "alsand sandbox deploys are MANUAL (no auto Cloud Build trigger); backend containers gate startup on `npm test` so a hanging/failing test 404s new routes" metadata: type: project --- The **albert sandbox** (alsand, `api.alsand.designflow.app`) runs on GCP project `lithe-breaker-323913`, region `us-central1`. Cloud Run services: `popcre-albert-{frontend,backend,bff,item,tracking,sync}-sandbox`. **Deploys are MANUAL — pushing `sandbox-albert` does NOT auto-deploy.** There are no GitHub→Cloud Build triggers (the builds that exist are manual `gcloud builds submit` runs; trigger list is empty). To deploy a service after pushing: 1. Build+push image (Dockerfile takes `--build-arg NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert`; image path `us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/lithe-breaker-323913/popcre-albert-sandbox/sandbox-albert/:`). 2. `gcloud run deploy --image= --region=us-central1` — `--image`-only keeps existing env/secrets/SA(`deployer@…`)/vpc-connector(`creatiflow`)/scaling, lowest risk. Add a `.gcloudignore` (node_modules is NOT gitignored) so the upload stays lean. **Recurring trap — backend containers gate startup on the test suite.** Dockerfile `CMD` is `yarn start:$NODE_ENV`, and every `start:*` script is `npm test && … nodemon index.js` (item-master, likely tracking/backend too). If `npm test` hangs (jest open handle) or fails, the `&&` never reaches the server, the container never listens on its PORT, the Cloud Run startup probe fails (`HealthCheckContainerError`), and **traffic stays pinned to the previous healthy revision** — so newly-added routes return **404** even though the code and image are correct. Symptom seen 2026-06-15: item library toast `HTTP 404 /api/item_master/lib/getGridCellNotes` while the route existed in source. **Triage:** `gcloud run revisions list --service= --region=us-central1` → look for the newest revision with STATUS `False`; `gcloud run services describe … --format='value(status.traffic)'` shows traffic stuck on an older revision; read the failed revision's logs (`gcloud logging read 'resource.labels.revision_name="…"'`) for the jest "did not exit" line. **Fix applied:** `test` → `jest --forceExit` in item-master `package.json`. If a future service has the same hang, do the same (or move the test gate out of the container start). See [[feedback_branch_pr_flow]] (ship the fix via PR sandbox-albert→develop).

lastPrompt: now when loading the itemlibrary page i get this toast error: Get Grid Cell Notes could not find the requested resource. Technical details: HTTP 404; URL: https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/item_ma…

content.text:
Fixed and verified. Here's what was going on.

## Root cause (not the redesign)
The toast was a deploy failure in the **item-master backend**, surfaced — not caused — by unrelated frontend activity. The chain:

1. The `/lib/getGridCellNotes` (+ `setGridCellNote`) routes were added to item-master `sandbox-albert` on 06-13, but **alsand deploys are manual** — no auto-trigger fires on push.
2. A manual deploy *was* attempted (revision **00012**, 06-14), but it **failed the Cloud Run health check**. Cloud Run kept 100% of traffic on the previous healthy revision **00011** (06-11), which predates those routes → **404**.
3. Why 00012 failed: the container's `CMD` is `yarn start:$NODE_ENV` → `npm test && … nodemon index.js`. After the cell-notes tests were added, **jest leaves an open handle and never exits**, so the `&&` never starts the server → the container never listens on `PORT=5003` → probe fails. (Confirmed in the revision logs: *"Jest did not exit one second after the test run has completed"* → startup TCP probe failed.)

I confirmed it precisely: the sibling route `getItemTypes` returned 403 (reachable) while `getGridCellNotes` returned 404 (route absent on the serving revision).

## The fix
- **[designflow-item-master/package.json](designflow-item-master/package.json)**: `"test": "jest --forceExit"` — the tests pass; this just guarantees jest terminates so the startup gate completes and the server boots. Commit `dc135a9`.
- Added **`.gcloudignore`** (`node_modules` wasn't gitignored) so manual builds stay lean. Commit `bbbc872`.
- **Built + deployed** to `popcre-albert-item-sandbox` (us-central1), keeping all existing env/secrets/config (`--image`-only deploy).

## Verified live
- `getGridCellNotes` / `setGridCellNote` → now **403** (route exists, auth-protected) instead of 404 → with the app's auth token they return 200, so **the toast is gone**.
- New revision **00014-cj8**: **healthy**, serving 100% of traffic.
- PR [item-master#22](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-item-master/pull/22) (sandbox-albert → develop) updated with the fix; saved the deploy mechanism + this trap to memory.

## Two things worth flagging
- **This is independent of the item-detail redesign** — different repo, pre-existing breakage. The redesign commit is on `sandbox-albert` but **not yet deployed** to the frontend (deploys are manual here; last frontend deploy was 06-12). Say the word and I'll build + deploy `popcre-albert-frontend-sandbox` so you can see the redesign live.
- The deeper fragility: gating a container's startup on its own test suite means any hung/failing test takes the service down. `--forceExit` fixes the immediate hang, but moving the test run out of the container start (into CI/the Docker build) would be the durable fix — happy to do that as a follow-up.

What does this mean "The redesign commit is on sandbox-albert but not yet deployed to the frontend (deploys are manual here; last frontend deploy was 06-12)"? I'm not allowed to touch MAIN or develop. I just want this on sandbox-albert on github.com.

message.content:
<system-reminder>Message sent at Mon 2026-06-15 12:54:12 UTC.</system-reminder>
What does this mean "The redesign commit is on sandbox-albert but not yet deployed to the frontend (deploys are manual here; last frontend deploy was 06-12)"? I'm not allowed to touch MAIN or develop. I just want this on sandbox-albert on github.com.

content.text:
Good question — and the short answer is: **what you want is already done.** Let me untangle the two separate things, because "deploy" here does **not** mean main or develop.

There are two independent concepts in this setup:

1. **Git branch (github.com)** — where the *code* lives. Your redesign is committed and pushed to `sandbox-albert` on github.com (commit `c9b22f95`, push `86d641a2..c9b22f95`). **Nothing touched `main` or `develop`** — the only thing I did with `develop` was earlier read-only sync, and the PR just *targets* `develop` without merging. So this is exactly where you asked it to be. ✅

2. **Deployment (Cloud Run)** — a *separate* step that takes the code, builds it into a running container, and publishes it to the **live sandbox website** (the `alsand` / `api.alsand.designflow.app` server). This has nothing to do with git branches. In this project it's **manual** — pushing to `sandbox-albert` does **not** automatically update the running site.

So when I said "not yet deployed," I only meant: **the live sandbox website hasn't been rebuilt since 06-12, so if you open the sandbox site in a browser, you won't *see* the new design yet** — even though the code is correctly on `sandbox-albert`.

The item-master fix from the last message *did* require a deploy because the bug was in the running server (a 404). The redesign doesn't *require* a deploy to be "on sandbox-albert" — it's already there. A deploy would only be needed if you want to **view it rendered on the live sandbox site**.

So:
- **If you just wanted the code on `sandbox-albert` (github.com)** → done, nothing more needed, and `main`/`develop` were never touched.
- **If you also want to see it live on the sandbox website** → that's the optional frontend deploy (build + `gcloud run deploy popcre-albert-frontend-sandbox`), same kind of step I did for item-master. I won't do that unless you ask.

Want me to deploy the frontend so you can see the redesign live, or leave it as just-committed for now?
