content.text:
The layout revision is built and pending compile verification. Here's a summary of what I changed to address each point — I'll commit to `sandbox-albert` as soon as the build confirms clean:

**Header → one line:** status pills + item name + Style#/Reference#/Art# now flow horizontally on a single row (wrapping only if the window is narrow), with actions on the right. Padding tightened.

**Image too big → ~50% smaller, info to its right:** the Main tab is now a true two-column layout — a compact **320px left column** (product image + "At a glance" stacked) and the **Item Attributes card to the right** filling the remaining width. The gallery hero is capped (≈250px tall) so it scales down instead of dominating.

**Wasted space → packed:** the attribute grid now uses `auto-fill` columns (`minmax(185px, 1fr)`), so it fits as many readable columns as the space allows (3–4 across) instead of collapsing to 2 wide columns with big empty gaps. Inter-section gaps and the timeline band height were tightened.

**Fonts:** bumped field values to 14px and labels to 11px for small-screen readability; the mobile breakpoints no longer shrink the grid below readable sizes.

Specs already pass (11/11). Once the build returns clean I'll commit + push to `sandbox-albert` (which also updates PR #124) — no `main`/`develop` involved. I'll report back when it lands.

content.content:
AGENTS.md           | 34 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 HANDOFF.md          | 11 +++++++++++

 4 files changed, 66 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
=== short diff preview ===
diff --git a/AGENTS.md b/AGENTS.md
index 92e31786..e570ea09 100644
--- a/AGENTS.md
+++ b/AGENTS.md
@@ -490,6 +490,28 @@ The following directories exist in `src/app/pages/` but are legacy/demo scaffold
 
 ---
 
+### The RFQ grid uses AG Grid's Theming API (`themeQuartz`), NOT the legacy CSS theme
+
+**Looks like:** `rfq.component.html` sets `class="ag-theme-alpine"` and `angular.json` loads `ag-theme-alpine.css`, so it looks like a legacy CSS-themed grid you can restyle with `.ag-theme-alpine { --ag-*: ... }` overrides.
+
+**Actually:** The grid runs on AG Grid v35's **Theming API**. `GridUISetupService.bootstrap()` (`src/@vex/services/grid-ui.service.ts`, called from `onGridReady`) does `gridApi.setGridOption('theme', <theme>)` from `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` (`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/grid-themes.config.ts`, default `themeQuartz`). The rendered grid element carries machine-generated classes (`ag-theme-params-1`, `ag-theme-buttonStyle-1`, ...); the `ag-theme-alpine` class is inert and legacy `--ag-*` CSS-variable overrides do **not** recolor it.
+
+**Why:** Verified 2026-06-15 — a redesign attempt overrode `.ag-theme-alpine` variables and computed styles showed zero effect; the live grid element had no `ag-theme-alpine` class.
+
+**Future sessions should:** To restyle the grid's palette/typography, build a `Theme` with `themeQuartz.withParams({...})` and apply it (e.g. `setGridOption('theme', ...)` after `bootstrap()`, RFQ-scoped, so the shared theme service and other grids are unaffected). Structural CSS (row/header heights, borders, the frozen seam) still works via `::ng-deep` on **real** AG classes (`.ag-header-group-cell`, `.ag-cell-last-left-pinned`, `.ag-header-row-group/-column/-filter`). Pills, tinted cells, and custom buttons need cell/header renderers — they are not theme params.
+
+---
+
+### Saved Views (backend column-state) override colDef defaults on load
+
+**Looks like:** Setting `pinned` / `width` / `hide` / order on a colDef in `rfq.grid.config.ts` should be the on-screen default.
+
+**Actually:** On load, `applyGridViewStateForViewName()` calls `gridApi.applyColumnState({ state, applyOrder: true })` using the backend `GridLayout` view-state, which overrides colDef `pinned`/`width`/order. Verified 2026-06-15: a colDef change to pin Picture/Description (and new widths) did nothing live — `getColumnState()` showed the saved view's old `pinned: null` / widths winning.
+
+**Future sessions should:** If a layout default must always apply, force it after the view restore (`applyColumnState` in `onGridReady` after the view loads) or update the saved Default view. A colDef default alone won't stick for users with a saved view.
+
+---
+
 ## 12. Credentials and environment
 
 All API base URLs are compiled into the Angular bundle at build time via `src/environments/`. There are no runtime `.env` files in the frontend container.
@@ -535,6 +557,18 @@ See `docs/deployment.md` for the full pipeline. Summary:
 
 ## 14. Critical incident log
 
+### 2026-06-15 — Stale `index.html` cache served an old frontend (empty RFQ sub-grids after deploy)
+
+**What happened:** After a backend change to how RFQ factory quotes load (a `develop` "RFQ performance" refactor → lazy per-row vendor loading via a new `getRfqItemVendors` endpoint), users saw empty detail sub-grids even though the `Res` column showed quote counts, and the page looked unchanged after frontend deploys.
+
+**Root cause:** `nginx.conf` served `index.html` with a 30-day cache (the catch-all `location ~* \.(js|css|pdf|html|swf)$ { expires 30d }` block included `html`). Browsers kept running a **cached old frontend** that loads quotes the old (eager) way; against the new lazy backend it rendered empty. The deployed app was actually correct and matched — the browser was stale.
+
+**Recovery:** Fixed `nginx.conf` (`7e9ca2d7`): `index.html` is now `Cache-Control: no-cache` via an exact-match `location = /index.html` block (security headers restated there since an `add_header` in a location replaces inherited ones), and `html` was removed from the long-cache bucket; content-hashed JS/CSS stay long-cached. Currently-cached browsers need one hard refresh; afterward, deploys reach everyone immediately. The triggering backend merge was reverted on `sandbox-albert` (see `designflow-backend` §14). Detail: `docs/deployment.md → Static asset caching`.
+

+## Static asset caching (nginx)
+
+Caching policy lives in `nginx.conf`. The rule that matters:
+
+- **`index.html` must never be long-cached.** It is the SPA entry point that references the build's content-hashed JS/CSS; if browsers cache it, they keep running an old app after a deploy. It is served `Cache-Control: no-cache` via an exact-match `location = /index.html` block (the browser stores it but revalidates every load → cheap 304s, instant pickup of new builds). Security headers (`X-Frame-Options`, etc.) are restated inside that block because an `add_header` in a location replaces the inherited http-level headers.
+- **Content-hashed assets (`.js`, `.css`, images, `woff2`) are long-cached** — their filenames change every build, so this is safe.
+- Do **not** add `html` back to the long-cache (`expires 30d`) bucket. Doing so re-creates the 2026-06-15 incident (stale frontend → empty RFQ sub-grids after a backend rollout). See `AGENTS.md §14`.
+
+Note: a browser that cached `index.html` *before* this fix deployed will still honor its old 30-day cache until it expires or the user hard-refreshes once; the fix prevents recurrence going forward.

+### The RFQ grid uses AG Grid's Theming API (`themeQuartz`), NOT the legacy CSS theme
+
+**Looks like:** `rfq.component.html` sets `class="ag-theme-alpine"` and `angular.json` loads `ag-theme-alpine.css`, so it looks like a legacy CSS-themed grid you can restyle with `.ag-theme-alpine { --ag-*: ... }` overrides.
+
+**Actually:** The grid runs on AG Grid v35's **Theming API**. `GridUISetupService.bootstrap()` (`src/@vex/services/grid-ui.service.ts`, called from `onGridReady`) does `gridApi.setGridOption('theme', <theme>)` from `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` (`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/grid-themes.config.ts`, default `themeQuartz`). The rendered grid element carries machine-generated classes (`ag-theme-params-1`, `ag-theme-buttonStyle-1`, ...); the `ag-theme-alpine` class is inert and legacy `--ag-*` CSS-variable overrides do **not** recolor it.
+
+**Why:** Verified 2026-06-15 — a redesign attempt overrode `.ag-theme-alpine` variables and computed styles showed zero effect; the live grid element had no `ag-theme-alpine` class.
+
+**Future sessions should:** To restyle the grid's palette/typography, build a `Theme` with `themeQuartz.withParams({...})` and apply it (e.g. `setGridOption('theme', ...)` after `bootstrap()`, RFQ-scoped, so the shared theme service and other grids are unaffected). Structural CSS (row/header heights, borders, the frozen seam) still works via `::ng-deep` on **real** AG classes (`.ag-header-group-cell`, `.ag-cell-last-left-pinned`, `.ag-header-row-group/-column/-filter`). Pills, tinted cells, and custom buttons need cell/header renderers — they are not theme params.
+
+---
+
+### Saved Views (backend column-state) override colDef defaults on load
+
+**Looks like:** Setting `pinned` / `width` / `hide` / order on a colDef in `rfq.grid.config.ts` should be the on-screen default.
+
+**Actually:** On load, `applyGridViewStateForViewName()` calls `gridApi.applyColumnState({ state, applyOrder: true })` using the backend `GridLayout` view-state, which overrides colDef `pinned`/`width`/order. Verified 2026-06-15: a colDef change to pin Picture/Description (and new widths) did nothing live — `getColumnState()` showed the saved view's old `pinned: null` / widths winning.
+
+**Future sessions should:** If a layout default must always apply, force it after the view restore (`applyColumnState` in `onGridReady` after the view loads) or update the saved Default view. A colDef default alone won't stick for users with a saved view.

 ## 14. Critical incident log
 
+### 2026-06-15 — Stale `index.html` cache served an old frontend (empty RFQ sub-grids after deploy)
+
+**What happened:** After a backend change to how RFQ factory quotes load (a `develop` "RFQ performance" refactor → lazy per-row vendor loading via a new `getRfqItemVendors` endpoint), users saw empty detail sub-grids even though the `Res` column showed quote counts, and the page looked unchanged after frontend deploys.
+
+**Root cause:** `nginx.conf` served `index.html` with a 30-day cache (the catch-all `location ~* \.(js|css|pdf|html|swf)$ { expires 30d }` block included `html`). Browsers kept running a **cached old frontend** that loads quotes the old (eager) way; against the new lazy backend it rendered empty. The deployed app was actually correct and matched — the browser was stale.
+
+**Recovery:** Fixed `nginx.conf` (`7e9ca2d7`): `index.html` is now `Cache-Control: no-cache` via an exact-match `location = /index.html` block (security headers restated there since an `add_header` in a location replaces inherited ones), and `html` was removed from the long-cache bucket; content-hashed JS/CSS stay long-cached. Currently-cached browsers need one hard refresh; afterward, deploys reach everyone immediately. The triggering backend merge was reverted on `sandbox-albert` (see `designflow-backend` §14). Detail: `docs/deployment.md → Static asset caching`.
+
+**Rule added:** Never long-cache `index.html` (or any SPA entry HTML) — cache only content-hashed assets. Any rollout that changes a frontend↔backend data contract (e.g. lazy loading) will break browsers pinned to a stale entry page until the cache fix is deployed AND they reload.
+
+---
+
 ### 2025 — RFQ calculations silently broken by `aggFunc` on Date Modified
 
 **What happened:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to the Date Modified column (commit `43db88d`) activated AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline. All group rows lost `params.data` (became `undefined`) in every valueSetter and valueGetter.
